title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the CC genotype (do not carry a copy of the CFTR R74W variant) and cystic fibrosis have an unknown response to ivacaftor treatment, as response may depend on the presence of other CFTR variants. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
riskSummary: CFTR variant associated with efficacy for ivacaftor
detail: Patients with the CC genotype (do not carry a copy of the CFTR R74W variant) and cystic fibrosis have an unknown response to ivacaftor treatment, as response may depend on the presence of other CFTR variants. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
whyItMatters: PharmGKB curates pharmacogenomic evidence from the literature, with level 1A–2B annotations backed by expert review and published clinical studies.
notes: PharmGKB annotations reflect published literature and should not replace clinical pharmacogenomic testing.

title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the CT genotype (one copy of the CFTR R74W variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. FDA-approved drug labeling information and CPIC guidelines indicate use of ivacaftor in cystic fibrosis patients with at least one copy of R74W. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.

title: Lower lung cancer and increased breast cancer risk
summary: Lower lung cancer and increased breast cancer risk
detail: rs664677 polymorphism was associated with decreased lung cancer risk and increased breast cancer risk (for lung cancer: TC vs. TT: OR = 0.76, 95% CI 0.62-0.92, CC vs. TT: OR = 0.80, 95% CI 0.64-0.99 and for breast cancer: TC vs. TT: OR = 1.42, 95% CI 1.17-1.73, CC vs. TT: OR = 1.51, 95% CI 1.21-1.87). [PMID 22203481]
